column1: App notifications/form submission
column30:
Right now, the app is giving users a "1" badge on their home screen, but once they open the app, there's no trail to follow. I’ve got a user (Tonya) who is getting frustrated because she sees the notification, but then has to scroll through every menu or check her email just to figure out what happened.

Specifically, when someone fills out a testimonial form, she has no idea where to find that in the app.

*How it should work:*

* *Take them there:* If they tap the notification, it should open the app directly to that specific form submission or the contact card. No more hunting.
* *Show a "Red Dot":* We need a visual cue inside the menu (like on "Contacts" or "Form Submissions") so they know where the new stuff is.
* *Dashboard visibility:* These submissions should probably show up in the "Recent Activity" feed on the dashboard so they’re front and center. Or a dedicated ‘Form Submissions’ section could work.

column30:
Customers want to place a signup form (e.g., newsletter opt-ins, contact forms) inside a shared footer so it appears on every page of their site. Currently, the {{[form]}} tag only renders when placed directly on an individual page. When a footer containing a {{[form]}} tag is applied globally, the system displays the literal {{[form]}} text instead of the rendered form.

This feature request proposes allowing {{[form]}} tags to function inside footer pages and other shared components, enabling forms to be displayed consistently across multiple pages without requiring page-by-page placement.

column1: hide full price payment options on pages when coupon code is used
column30:
Ideally, this would involve changing page payment options so that an item for sale is bundled or otherwise connected to its coupon-powered discounted option. Currently, coupons simply reveal new payment options when entered. With such a change, entering a coupon code would remove the full priced listing *and* reveal the same item at its discounted rate. 

Example: A sales page offers a course for 297. A 30% off coupon code entered on this page _changes the price of the item_ from 297 to 197.99. 

Currently, entering the coupon code _only adds the new payment option_ of 197.99. 

When adding multiple payment options (and coupon codes for those), this creates confusion and visual clutter. See image.

column1: Investigate whether Stripe’s API allows support for additional payment options similar to Square
column30:
Customer asked for alternative payment methods (e.g., Square) to reduce card fees, enable in-person payments, or provide more flexibility for bookings. 

*Does Stripe allow for:*

# *Does Stripe’s API support additional card-present or cash-based flows* that customers could use in place of Square for in-person classes or bookings?
# *Does Stripe support “reserve with card, pay in person” workflows* (authorize only, capture later) similar to common fitness-class booking systems?
# *Can Stripe allow customer-controlled payment methods* that resemble Square’s convenience for wellness businesses (e.g., lower-fee options, ACH push, or wallet-based payments that customers prefer)?
# *Does Stripe have built-in mechanisms to reduce or bypass card fees* in situations where customers pay cash in person but still want to hold a card on file?
# *What does Stripe’s roadmap or documentation say about expanding their payment options* that could eliminate the need for Square altogether?

Add automated course completion certificates and video seek restriction controls
column30:
# Automatically generate and send a *personalized PDF certificate* upon course completion (including student name, date completed, and course title).
# Restrict video playback controls within courses to *prevent fast-forwarding or skipping ahead*, ensuring compliance for continuing education (CE) requirements.

*Context:*

Dr. Talbert is creating accredited continuing education courses for healthcare providers. CE regulations require verifiable proof of completion and prohibit skipping through video material. Currently, these functions must be handled manually or through external tools (e.g., Zapier, Google Sheets, or PDFEscape).

*Current Behavior:*

* Certificates must be created and sent manually.
* Videos hosted in AttractWell can be fast-forwarded.
* No way to securely store generated certificates per contact without manual upload.

*Proposed Solution:*

* Add an automation option within Course or Vault settings to generate a PDF certificate template with merge fields for:

** {{contact_name}}
** {{course_name}}
** {{completion_date}}
* 
* Store the generated certificate in the contact’s *Files* section automatically and send it as an email attachment upon completion.
* Add an optional toggle in course video settings to disable seeking/fast-forwarding.

column1: Add custom Home page for member area
Add Campaign Message Timeline View Showing Actual Send Dates
column30:
Users running long campaigns can’t quickly see the actual calendar dates messages were (or will be) sent. The UI exposes:

* *Planned dates* only when campaign start = “On a specific date” (editor view).
* *Per-contact history* with relative times (e.g., “3 days ago”) and sequence numbers (“Day 63”) but *no absolute dates* and *no campaign-level overview*.
This forces users to cross-reference inbox timestamps and manually count days to find “where we are now.”

*User Story*
As a campaign owner, I want a campaign-level timeline that maps each message’s sequence day to a real calendar date (past and future), so I can audit progress and plan changes without manual date math or opening multiple screens.

*Context (Customer Signal)*
Customer: *Mika Capitana* (email thread 2025-10-17 to 2025-10-18).
Key quotes (paraphrased): wants to see “Sent on [date]” and understand “Which day of the campaign is today in real time?” The Message History view is found but feels insufficient: relative times and scattered details make it hard to get temporal context.

*Current Behavior*

* Campaign editor shows computed dates only when start is a specific date (screenshot provided).
* Contact card → Message History lists messages with sequence labels and relative timestamps; lacks absolute dates and aggregate view across recipients.

*Desired Behavior (High-Level)*

* Provide a *campaign-level timeline* (read-only) that:
*# Shows *absolute calendar dates* for each message (e.g., “Sent on Oct 5, 2025 at 12:00 PM Central” or “Scheduled for Oct 21, 2025”).
*# Indicates *status* per message (Scheduled, Sending, Sent, Skipped/Failed).
*# Highlights *“Today”* position (e.g., “We are on Day 64 → Oct 21, 2025”).
*# Works whether the campaign starts “ASAP” or on a *specific date* (compute dates per recipient where needed; see scope notes).
*# Offers a toggle for *Absolute date ↔ Relative* (“3 days ago”) to reduce cognitive load.
*# Optional: export/printable summary.

column1: Add confetti.js to builder
Add alert for broken or mismatched custom mail servers
column30:
h3. *Summary:*

Users are unaware when their configured custom mail server becomes invalid after changing their sending email address or disconnecting a domain. This results in broken links (e.g., Vault URLs pointing to inactive domains) and failed email delivery.

We already display an alert when a user’s email address isn’t verified. The same UX pattern could be used here — notifying users that their custom mail server is no longer active or mismatched with their account email.

----

h3. *Background / Context:*

Recent support tickets show recurring confusion and functional failures tied to mismatched or disconnected mail servers:

* *10/16/2025 — Jill Kearns:*
Custom mail server on [_jillkearns.com_|http://jillkearns.com] broke after she switched to _thoughtful.email_. The app continued referencing the old domain without alerting her.
* *10/16/2025 — Ellena Mendoza:*
Vault and Zoom links failed because her mail server still pointed to [_betteryourbiome.com_|http://betteryourbiome.com] after updating to [_radiantrootsfunctionalwellness.com_|http://radiantrootsfunctionalwellness.com]. No alert indicated that her mail server was broken.

Both cases show a lack of in-app feedback when the sending domain and mail server configuration fall out of sync.

----

h3. *Proposed Solution:*

Add a system alert or banner (similar to the “email not verified” notice) that appears when any of the following are true:

# The domain on the configured custom mail server doesn’t match the user’s active sending email domain.
# The mail server configuration is missing or invalid.
# The mail server was automatically disabled due to a mismatch or DNS failure.

*Recommended behavior:*

* Display a clear warning banner:
“Your custom mail server for [domain] is inactive or mismatched. Please email support to reconnect it.”
* Bonus points if it can automatically revert to our default mail server. Then the alert could read as: 
* “Your custom mail server for [domain] is inactive or mismatched. Emails will be sent from AttractWell’s default servers until it’s reconnected.”
* I dont really think this would work without Mailguns API, but include a one-click “Reconnect Mail Server” link to reopen the setup flow.

----

column1: Gift Month / Skip Billing for Memberships
column30:
{panel:bgColor=#deebff}
It should be noted that I dont know the left mouse button from the right mouse button when it comes to this sort of thing. But if there is anything of value here, it would be great to be able to offer as a feature. 
{panel}

Creators need a native way to grant a *free month* (or skip the next billing cycle) to individual or bulk members *without leaving AttractWell* and *without breaking vault access*. Stripe does not support $0 charges, but it supports $0 invoices via discounts/schedules. Implement a first-class “Gift Month / Skip Billing” capability that:

# Discounts the next invoice to $0 while keeping the subscription active, and
# Keeps vault access continuous and auditable, and
# Works for one-off or bulk actions.

h2. *Technical Approach*

h3. *Pattern A (MVP) —* 

h3. *100% OFF Once Discount on Next Invoice*

* Create (once) or reuse a system coupon: percent_off: 100, duration: "once".
* Apply to subscription via stripe.subscriptions.update(sub_id, { discounts: [{coupon: COUPON_ID}], proration_behavior: "none" }).
* Result: Next invoice is $0 → finalizes → subscription remains active → following cycle bills normally.
* Pros: Simple, robust, matches most use cases (e.g., Dara’s “18 members get 1 free month”).
* Webhook handling:
** On invoice.finalized / invoice.paid with amount_due == 0 → flag as *gifted cycle*; do not change access.
** On normal (non-zero) invoice failure → follow existing dunning/access rules.

h3. *Pattern B (Phase control) —* 

h3. *Subscription Schedule with a Gift Phase*

* For date-driven or multi-month gifts, create a *Subscription Schedule* with phases:
*# Phase 1: 1 iteration with 100% discount.
*# Phase 2: back to normal price.
* Use for planned promos or precise timing.
* Same webhook + access rules.

{quote}Note: Avoid pause_collection for this use case; discount-to-zero keeps invoices and lifecycle consistent.{quote}

column1: Syndication ideas
column30:
Syndication wish list ideas from a potential customer, [kbrown088@outlook.com|mailto:kbrown088@outlook.com] Kelli Brown. She volunteered to provide feedback on it if we develop this.



* Automations for social media, so it's a one click and boom the post goes out to all social media platforms that you have
* Podcasting services like what buzz sprout does - you record using your recording tools, then upload to the service and publish, it takes are of posting the episodes to all the podcasting services like Apple Podcasts, Spotify, amazing etc.
* website migration
* YouTube integration, so that as I do video podcasting the published video would go straight to youtube or Vimeo etc.



Some notes from her about syndication:
_I record my podcast on Logic Pro on my Mac (currently, might move to Davinci). I edit in Logic Pro and when I'm satisfied, I just upload my recording to buzzspout. It runs through a process where it does some minor sound clarification but I also use their AI service too, which creates my title and episode description for me. It will even create a blog post from the recording. It gives me 5 title options (or I can use my own) and the episode description (which I can edit) and when I'm satisfied I can either schedule the publish date or I can publish right away. and when it publishes it syndicates and sends out to the podcast apps.  It will even create social media snippets for me but like attract well I have to click the button to post them._

_You got most of what a coach and a content creator would need, just need the automation and some content stuff for podcasting etc. and you're on top of the world_

Use case: 

Momentum workshops were set up with “booking types” feature, where up to 20 contacts could select a given time slot. A “workshop booked” tag is applied via an automation at the time of booking to all contacts. 

Aside from automated emails/texts set up at booking, there is currently no way to identify and communicate with contacts based on time slot selected aside from manually entering contact names into a spreadsheet by date, manually selecting those contacts in system and bulk applying a tag.

For users offering group programs with multiple date choices, it would be tremendously helpful to allow an easier way to group these cohorts. A “select contacts” feature with “bulk actions” option in “booked meetings” list could help tremendously to this end. See screenshot for mockup idea.

Add "group meeting" option to call packages
column30:
Customer use case: coaching package where multiple parties would join (such as when selling to organizations, teams, families)

!IMG_0042.jpeg|thumbnail!



Internal use case - scaling high-touch group programs like momentum in GO: 

A group coaching program serves dozens of clients in the same vault. To best accommodate scheduling and hands-on help for the (one) quarterly 4-hour workshop being offered to each client who purchased, 4 workshop date/time combos are offered to choose from. 

Currently, there is no automated way to ensure that these clients do not book themselves for multiple workshops. 

If group meeting is an option for call packages, our clients can offer paid or program-inclusive group calls on a recurring basis, and ensure that the right people book the right number of times when they do.

column30:
*Per-Date Availability Option*

# Ability to set availability for _specific dates_ (e.g., 1–2 select Mondays per month) rather than relying on weekly recurring schedules.
# This is currently only possible via a workaround using weekly availability + “Not Available On” blocks, which is not intuitive.

*“All Day” Option*

# Add a selectable “All Day” checkbox for availability settings, particularly in the “Times I’m Not Available” section.
# Current implementation requires specifying start and end times, even when wanting to mark the full day as unavailable.

*Streamlined Input for Exceptions*

# When attempting to delete certain days for exceptions (e.g., days teeth cleanings are _not_ offered), the system requires all fields to be filled in, causing frustration.

*Clarification Between GetOiling vs. Google Booking*

# The customer was considering using Google Calendar’s booking page instead but ultimately prefers to stay within GetOiling for consistency and automation, provided the specific-date option is implemented.

column1: redirect or notification that the appointment cannot be cancelled
column30:
Since this is a long email thread, here is the section with her request:

I have set the minimum cancellation as 1 hour on some appointments and 24 hours on others. I noticed that if someone tries cancels their appointment via the link in their confirmation email with insufficient notice time, the link simply redirects them to my home page and does not give any message to say that they are not able to cancel their appointment with short notice. 

I think also this may happen when someone tries to cancel within the notice period also as I have had clients cancelling and then checking they had been cancelled. 

How do I set this up so they receive an onscreen message and/or a cancellation email to inform them that they have either cancelled their appointment or are unable to cancel due to short notice?

column1: A “New Contacts/Leads This Week” feature in Contact Manager
column30:
It would be very helpful to be able to see a snapshot of (or even have a space in the dashboard that highlights this) new leads, week by week. This could help users quickly take action on initiating and moving new conversations forward. 

To differentiate from just “new” contacts, our users could specify what tags allow a contact to show up in such a field. For instance, they may have a general mailing list funnel that does not necessitate a contact showing up in a field like this—but new leads for a high ticket or network marketing funnel necessitate more action. This would be of particularly high value to network marketers or anyone focusing on growing their network and prospecting—giving them a quick visual cue as to how many new contacts they’ve made this week and who to focus on immediately outside of ongoing maintenance tasks.

I know we have contact ratings, search features and follow up plans for this, but I think many users would benefit from having a dummy-proof way to quickly (visually) differentiate leads that need more immediate action.

column1: Auto-fill contact data (at least name, email) on forms
column30:
This would be ideal in instances where a campaign provides a CTA with link to fill out a form on our user’s website (such as an intake form, pre-call form, member area account setup or registration for events, calls etc), as well as redirects from purchase, booking or other registration.

In some instances, all form or booking-based functions can’t or shouldn’t be fulfilled on a single page/form, and the end user needs to fill out a form on a subsequent page/at a subsequent step of the process. Carrying over name, email from previous step would provide a better user experience and prevent issues arising from duplicate contacts because a wrong email address was used, was mistyped, etc

column1: Vault Feedback
column30:
Good morning Greg,

I am designing my Vault for a 30-day challenge that I wanted to open up on Monday 11 (this could go to Tues or Wed I suppose) with the first class on the November 19th.

I cannot figure out how to design a vault member area as there aren’t any section headers, column sections, no ability to make it for phone reading like when designing a page.

Feedback on Vault:  The very first thing is pages, entry, and something else.  I started creating the pages.  When I viewed it, it was a simply a list.  I thought “_is this what a vault looks like; it’s as boring as can be.”_  So I start designing pictures to put in a page to represent a blog banner header.  After an hour or two I start going down your features and under “Advance” I see “Cover Photo” and another photo option.  I realize THIS is the “look for the vault”.  Why doesn’t “Advanced” read something like "Vault Member area Home Location” or something to the effect.  Why is the “container” last.  Wouldn’t it be the first thing one would design before adding things into the container?

Feedback in General:  A month or so ago, you asked me why I am not doing anything on my site, and if you could help me.  Outside of taking care of my father, life, and participating in my coaching certification, my time is limited.  But specifically to AttractWell, I would probably be gone already except for the Contact Data, email writing portion.  I am a very visual person, and filling in boxes to create leaves me stuck as I can’t visualize what I am creating.  In my opinion designing in Attractwell is cumbersome.  I moved over from ClickFunnels, and their design features are much more simple and visual.  *This is NOT to downgrade all the amazing features that you offer and the complete-package system you have (which is why I moved to Attractwell).*  Other people may not have an issue with the design features.  It’s simply me,  If you know anything about the Gallup Strengths Assessment, I am HIGH executing, so when it takes so long to design, I get frustrated.  I spent a bunch of time designing my page cover photos last night, and they could simply be buttons; Not everyone will have the time or desire to create these things (Actually I love to create stuff, but when in a time crunch, simple will do). Again, this is just me.

Now back to my vault.  This vault is for a beta challenge for me to get testimonials so I can go out and build a home page and about page with offers and things (I don’t want my home or about page viewable for the challenge as they are very incomplete).  The landing page link for the vault will be given to 2-3 friends to share with their friends.

I’m going to send you a PDF inspiration image of what I want my vault to look like.  It is clean, simple, and all “above the fold”.

Question:  How does one get the title bar of the member area to go ABOVE the cover photo.  I can’t move it up or down?  In the example the individual person’s login information is in the title bar which is efficient.

Question:  I want to edit the name of a page within the vault, and for the life of me, I can’t figure out how to change it?

Question:  I am having real trouble with the buttons.  Whenever I insert one, and go to change the name on the button from the color name to what I want the button to say, the cursor goes OUTSIDE of the button.  It’s like the button is not its own entity, but a part of the “word processing” text area.

Question:  How DO I make the vault area phone readable?

Anyway, I don’t know if there is any possibility for my vault to look like the example I am going to send you, but I sure would love it to be.

Have a great day,

Melodee

column1: Bookings: create a "times I'm available" area for adding extra one-off times onto the calendar
column30:
*Samaya (Traci) Hill to Everyone (1:10 PM)*

Will there ever be a date override option for calendars like Calendly has?

*Me to Everyone (1:10 PM)*

Hi Samaya! Can you tell me more about the date override?

*Samaya (Traci) Hill to Everyone (1:11 PM)*

The override allows you to hand pick all the dates with specific availability times. So instead of each Monday having specific hours and each Tues etc, you go through your whole calendar to put in your specific hours

*Me to Everyone (1:16 PM)*

@Samaya - So none of the available times are recurring, right? You’d just pick one-time-use dates & times that people can book, and keep adding to those dates/times over time?  And are those for 1:1 meetings?

*Samaya (Traci) Hill to Everyone (1:17 PM)*

they are for all my appointments. I don't have regular hours so I need to use dates, not days. In Calendly I go through every day for about 3 months and input each date and my availability. Kinda time consuming but necessary for me

*Me to Everyone (1:20 PM)*

That makes sense! So it’s sort of the opposite of the “times I’m not available” field, right?  You’d just put in times you are available, instead of times you’re not available.

*Samaya (Traci) Hill to Everyone (1:22 PM)*

Correct. I just need it completely customizable for the entire year. Some Sundays I'm available, some I'm not. Some Saturdays Im avaible all day and some Saturdays only 9-11. So each date needs its own availability

*Deni - a day ago (Wed, Nov 6 2024 at 12:18 PM)*

 To:"Greg Kilwein" <[support@attractwell.com|mailto:support@attractwell.com]>

Hey Greg!

Thanks SO much for following up with this. It could absolutely work, sir. However...for efficiency and effectiveness...I'd LOVE to put making recurring bookings in the hat of updates. I'm confident this is and will be a HUGE timesaver for coaches, a service for our clients, AND a "plus" feature for AW. Especially since other platforms offer this already. It's another benefit that adds tons of value to the new calendar feature in AW.



*Greg Kilwein replied a day ago (Wed, Nov 6 2024 at 12:53 PM)*

[To:deni@denicarruth.com|mailto:To:deni@denicarruth.com]

Hi Deni! Thanks for the feedback.

Would it actually be recurring or would most situations have an end date?  For example, would it be better to have a "schedule X number of meetings in a row, every Tuesday at 3 pm" vs "every Tuesday at 3pm until forever"?  



*Deni replied 21 hours ago (Wed, Nov 6 2024 at 5:28 PM)*

 To:"Greg Kilwein" <[support@attractwell.com|mailto:support@attractwell.com]>

Hey Greg!

Great question. It's actually a both/and situation. It's recurring UNTIL a certain date. Here's my personal scenario, with the "typical" guidelines:

A client signs up for my 12-mo program. 

We meet 2x/mo on Zoom, the 1st and 3rd weeks of each month = 24 sessions.

They choose Wed at 1p.m.

Once all is confirmed, I'll do this for the client after their first session, IF we haven't already established that on their enrollment call.

I'll want to schedule their sessions for the 1st and 3rd Wed for the next 12 months.

I'll need to either adjust up front for any holidays, etc, AND/OR schedule, then go back end and adjust accordingly.

I'll also need to make any adjustments along the way to accommodate either of our schedules.

The same would be true for my 6-mo program, with 12 call sessions. Make sense

Is that helpful?



*Greg Kilwein replied an hour ago (Thu, Nov 7 2024 at 2:41 PM)* 

[To:deni@denicarruth.com|mailto:To:deni@denicarruth.com]

Hi Deni!

Yes, that's helpful.

What would you think of a streamlined way of selecting multiple appointments in a row?  Like, a way to book all the calls in a call package at once, maybe showing a 12 month rolling calendar or something like that. The person doing the booking could start it off with picking "Wednesdays at 1pm" or "every other Wednesday at 1pm" or "2x/mo on Wednesdays at 1pm" (just as an example) and then it pre-selects all of those Wednesdays, then individual days/times could be edited if there are any needs to adjust it.



*Deni replied 5 minutes ago (Thu, Nov 7 2024 at 3:42 PM)*

To:"Greg Kilwein" <[support@attractwell.com|mailto:support@attractwell.com]>

Hey Greg!

That's (almost) exactly what I'm used to, and talking about. Once I created one appointment for the client by selecting "every 1st Thursday" at whatever time, until whatever date, that was set. Then I'd create another appointment by selecting "every 3rd Thursday" at whatever time, until whatever date, and that was set. Then, yes...make any adjustments. Both appointment dates and times would show up for that client, for the extent of their program.

column1: Bulk appointment booking for the account owner, particularly with a call package
column30:
For call packages, the suggestion is to allow the account owner to book all the calls in the package at once.

The use case is this: the contact will purchase a 6 call package but the account owner wants to book the times for the contact.  This would allow the account owner to input all 6 dates and times for each meeting and send out all the invites at once. 

This was an AttractWell Office Hours suggestion on Oct 31, 2024.

----

This can be done now if someone clicks the link to book each call in the call package. They can all be booked back-to-back.

Basically, only those contacts who have been granted access to a call package that has remaining calls on it can use the link to book. Contacts can be granted access to a call package for free (by hand, automation, custom page, and landing page) or via payment (page builder, vault via automation, or store purchase).

column1: Area on dashboard indicating contacts requiring attention in member area
column30:
Centralized dashboard location to view activity required to tend to member area. Actions needed for list: 

* contact requests approval for next lesson (this currently only sends an email, as far as I’m aware)
* contact uploads homework
* (optional) contact posts in member area or in discussion on lesson

This could show up inside its own space in the dashboard, similar to “events with to-do’s”, etc. This would likely need to be separate from the to-do’s list/section, as it would need to remain on dashboard as a to-do until resolved, as opposed to falling under a date-based list

column1: Ability to copy/duplicate vault from list of vaults
column30:
Currently, the only way to replicate a vault is to create a resource bundle, add your vault, then claim the vault from your own bundle. 

Request is to copy a vault, along with all its contents and settings, by clicking “copy” under a vault’s name in the list of vaults in a user account. It would be useful to prompt the user to re-name the vault copy before it is generated, to limit confusion

This would be of particular use for those with unlimited vaults & classes who wish to serve clients in private dashboard vaults (1 vault per client, copied from template vault they create for this purpose)

column1: ability to perform numeric functions in progress tracker
column30: The progress tracker currently supports alphabetical or numerical inputs. Request is for the ability to perform basic mathematic functions on numerical inputs, such as sum totals, percentages and averages of data added to table.

column1: Discussion Room Privileges - see description for more context
column30:
Could we get status options for the vaults?
for example: 
Admins (can remove other people’s posts, or approve posts before they are shared – tagged if there are issues, etc)

Contributors – rather than giving everyone in a vault the ability to create posts, what if only certain individuals (ie leaders) could create posts?

These things don’t necessarily require admin access to the back office, just certain privileges/responsibilities within a vault or vaults.

Ok, thanks for listening.  Santa has nothing on you guys!!

 

Jill Kearns

column68:
12/Jan/26 1:29 PM;712020:ca06282e-f38b-4efd-870c-fc743815ddae;From a call today with new pro annual member Paul Gatto: 

A way for URLs to self-destruct after a set amount of time. 

He wants to allow access to PDFs and video recordings for 30 days after a live webinar. 

I walked him through using vaults to support this use case, but since there is no interactive element beyond the one live call, it makes the workflow a bit too bloated. 

This could involve making a video shareable for X number of days or up to a specific date, or adding a feature into page settings that allows a URL redirect (in advanced settings) to be valid for X number of days or up to a specific date.

A page with a countdown timer would technically work to display video and downloads, but design-wise the countdown timer wouldn’t be desirable or a fit for his audience/use case.

column1: Scheduled recurring reminder message
column30:
Not sure how to do this exactly, but it would be great to have a message that could be scheduled to send as a recurring reminder. For example, a monthly loyalty rewards reminder or even a happy birthday message. These messages could be set to send out for each contact on a certain day of the week/month/year.

If they composed a Happy Birthday message, it could be set to send to this particular contact on that day every year. A loyalty rewards reminder could send a ‘canned response’ to a contact monthly each month on the 5th to remind them to change their order…

column1: Add automation to one-time messages
column30:
Suzie wants a way to schedule adding contacts to a campaign.

She has an evergreen campaign set up for supporting people during their surgery.  Since their surgery dates are all on different days, she will need to manually add them to the campaign the day before. A solution would be to schedule a one-time message on their surgery date, which would have an automation to add them to the follow-up campaign when the message is SENT (not on a link click).

I’m hoping this is possible since campaign messages have the ability to trigger an automation when sent.

column1: we're hoping to add in dynamic pages or blog list element for blog categories wherein only blogs that are in the same category appear on the specific section or page.
Email form responses
column30:
Here is Sonia’s request:

I have a campaign to send my parents of student clients to complete an intake form. Once they complete it, is there a way for there to be an automation an option for them to enter their child's email address so they can receive a copy of their parents responses?

My thoughts…

Since the form responses are sent to the AW/GO member, is it possible to also send a copy of the responses to the contact? If so, could they also add an email to send a copy to another person (in this instance, it would be sent to the student)

column1: Simplify payment option for a quicker checkout
column30:
Hi Jessica! Quick Question. Is there any way to simplify the payment option for people for quicker payments? 

We are doing a trade show this weekend; and folks want simple. We are finding that they don’t want to make a password or fill in their address etc. 

What I do want to get from them is just an email and phone number. That’s it.  Is there any way to simplify the payment process for them? 

Thank you for any insight and help you may have!
column68: 04/Jun/25 12:56 PM;712020:c228930a-5fd3-47f1-b8fa-137a1143e165;The password is no longer required. The address is needed for tax purposes and should only be displayed when needed (when tax is being collected or there is at least one physical product in the cart.)
column69: 04/Jun/25 12:58 PM;712020:c228930a-5fd3-47f1-b8fa-137a1143e165;I also emailed the customer back.
